Test Description: Default value check and write/read verification across defined GPIO registers using mask arrays and skip lists. Implements two phases: (1) DEFAULT VALUE_CHECK (chk_rst_val) and (2) WRITE & READ CHECK (chk_rd_wr).
Remarks:
- VRRW registers are skipped as indicated by skip arrays (comment: 'SKIPPING VRRW registers').
- Note in source: when reading default values the DIN value may become 1 automatically unless forced, affecting expected readback.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point: test_case()
1) Call chk_rst_val().
   1.1) For i from 0 to CNT-1 (CNT=49):
        - addr = addr_array[i].
        - If skip_rst_array[i] == 1: continue (skip default check for this address).
        - If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (address not readable).
        - data_rd = read_reg(addr) [READ].
        - data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE) [MODIFY mask].
        - If (data == default_value_array[i]): optional PASS print (under DEBUG_DISPLAY).
        - Else: def_fail_cnt++ and print failure with Addr, Expected, Read_data, DATA.
2) Call chk_rd_wr().
   2.1) Define chk_val[6] = {0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000}.
   2.2) For each pattern index j in 0..5:
        - data_wr = chk_val[j].
        - Write phase: For i from 0 to CNT-1:
          a) addr = addr_array[i].
          b) If skip_array[i] == 1: continue (skip write for this address).
          c) If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (address not writable).
          d) Else write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i])) [WRITE].
        - Read/verify phase: For i from 0 to CNT-1:
          a) addr = addr_array[i].
          b) If skip_array[i] == 1: continue (skip read for this address).
          c) If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (not writable; skip).
          d) If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (not readable; skip).
          e) data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i]) [READ+MASK].
          f) wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF).
          g) exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]) ).
          h) If (data_rd == exp_val): optional PASS print (under DEBUG_DISPLAY).
             Else: wr_fail_cnt++ and print write-read mismatch with addr, exp_val, data_rd.
3) Test completion:
   - If (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0): finish(1) [FAIL].
   - Else: finish(0) [PASS].
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Default value check: For each address where (skip_rst_array[i] != 1) and (read_mask_array[i] != 0), PASS when (read_reg(addr_array[i]) & 0xFFFFFFFE) == default_value_array[i]; else def_fail_cnt++ and log.
- Write/Read check for each pattern and address where not skipped and masks valid: PASS when (read_reg(addr_array[i]) & read_mask_array[i]) == ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | ((write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF) & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); else wr_fail_cnt++.
- Overall PASS when (def_fail_cnt == 0 && wr_fail_cnt == 0) leading to finish(0); otherwise finish(1).

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and service validation for GPIO pads 8..39 with per-pin enable, raw clear, ISR verification, and bounded wait timeouts.
Remarks:
- Known-state drive to all-high before and after ISR to avoid spurious levels.
- Arm wait (int_pend = 1) before generating edge to avoid race.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry points and order:
A) test_case()
  1) test_err = 0.
  2) GIC_EnableIRQ(87) if GPIO0 defined; GIC_EnableIRQ(88) if GPIO1 defined.
  3) Enable sysreg interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR) [WRITE].
  4) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE] to drive all pads high (known state).
  5) Configure per-pin for input + negedge + raw clear (32 pins): for i=0..31:
     - addr1 = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i * 4).
     - write_reg(addr1, (1<<20)|(1<<18)|(1<<16)) [WRITE: doe=1, neie=1, iclr=1].
     - wait_on(10).
  6) For each pin i=0..31 generate falling edge and wait with timeout:
     - wr_val = (1u << i).
     - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, wr_val) [WRITE W1C pre-clear].
     - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, wr_val) [WRITE enable only this bit].
     - wait_on(10).
     - int_pend = 1.
     - write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE prepare high].
     - wait_on(30).
     - write_reg(0xA0243FFC, ~wr_val) [WRITE create falling edge on bit i].
     - timeout = 5000; while (int_pend && timeout--) { wait_on(10); } [BUSY-WAIT with timeout].
     - if (timeout == 0) { printf timeout error; test_err++; }.
  7) finish(test_err).
B) Default_IRQHandler()
  8) local_wr = (1u << i); int_pend = 0.
  9) write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE return to known high].
 10) raddr = MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4); rdata = read_reg(raddr) [READ per-pin status].
 11) If ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) then test_err++ (DIN should be 0 for negedge).
 12) If ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) then:
     - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) [READ group sts].
     - If ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) test_err++.
     - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), (1<<20)|(1<<16)) [WRITE: doe=1, iclr=1].
     - write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, local_wr) [WRITE W1C].
     - rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) [READ verify clear].
     - If (rdata_grp != 0x0) test_err++.
     - If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(87).
       If GPIO1: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR); GIC_ClearIRQ(88).
    Else: test_err++.
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Timeout wait in test_case: PASS path when interrupt arrives before timeout (int_pend cleared in ISR); FAIL increments test_err when timeout==0.
- In ISR: For negedge, DIN check: PASS when ((read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) & 0x1) == 0); else test_err++.
- Group status set: PASS when (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & (1u<<i)) != 0; else test_err++.
- After per-pin and group clear: PASS when read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0; else test_err++.
- Overall PASS when test_err == 0 at finish(test_err).

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable across all pads (GPIO8..GPIO39), group enable, ISR service with per-pin raw clear, group status verification, sysreg clear verification, and bounded wait.
Remarks:
- Masks group interrupt during ISR to avoid re-entrancy.
- Uses bounded wait with timeout=2000 in main loop.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry points and order:
A) test_case()
  1) Enable GIC IRQ: 87 if GPIO0, 88 if GPIO1.
  2) Enable sysreg interrupt: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1, LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR or LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR) [WRITE].
  3) For i=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (i*4), 0x00020000) [WRITE: posedge enable bit17=1].
  4) wait_on(10).
  5) Configure input mode: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1,0x000000FF); GROUP2,3,4 similarly [WRITES].
  6) wait_on(10).
  7) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE enable group bits].
  8) For i=0..31 (per pin):
     - write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000) [WRITE low].
     - wait_on(10).
     - int_pend = 1.
     - write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE rising edge].
     - timeout=2000; while (int_pend==1 && --timeout>0) { wait_on(10); } [BUSY-WAIT].
     - If (timeout==0) { printf timeout; test_err++; break; }
     - write_reg(0xA0243FFC, 0x00000000) [WRITE low for next].
     - wait_on(10).
  9) finish(test_err).
B) Default_IRQHandler()
 10) wr_val = (1<<i); int_pend = 0.
 11) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) [READ group sts].
 12) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0x00000000) [WRITE mask during service].
 13) If ((rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0) { optional success print } else { printf error; test_err++; }.
 14) For j=0..31: write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + (j*4), 0x00010000) [WRITE per-pin raw clear iclr=1].
 15) wait_on(2).
 16) rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) [READ verify clear]. If (rdata_grp==0x0) success else error and test_err++.
 17) If GPIO0: write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR); rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); If ((rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR)!=0) test_err++.
     If GPIO1: analogous with GPIO1 macros.
 18) write_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, 0xFFFFFFFF) [WRITE re-enable group].
 19) GIC_ClearIRQ(87/88).
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- In main: timeout wait PASS when ISR clears int_pend before timeout; FAIL increments test_err on timeout.
- In ISR: Group interrupt observed: PASS when (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0; else FAIL.
- After per-pin raw clear: PASS when read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0; else FAIL.
- Sysreg clear verification: PASS when (read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1) & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO{0|1}_INTR) == 0 after write_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1, LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO{0|1}_INTR); else FAIL.
- Overall PASS when test_err == 0 at finish(test_err).
